frozen_components: LLMs (used for alignment and reranking without fine-tuning)
architecture.summary: A three-stage pipeline that enhances Knowledge Graph Reasoning (KGR) by leveraging LLM knowledge without fine-tuning. Stage 1 (knowledge alignment): aligns LLM knowledge to KG schema by associating unconnected nodes with semantic relations to enrich the KG. Stage 2 (KG reasoning): trains structure-aware KGR models on the enriched KG to integrate aligned knowledge with original KG knowledge. Stage 3 (entity reranking): reranks top-scored entities from KGR models using LLMs to recover correct answers. Information flows from LLM-extracted semantic relations → enriched KG structure → trained KGR model → LLM reranking of candidate outputs.

biblio.title: A New Pipeline for Knowledge Graph Reasoning Enhanced by Large Language Models Without Fine-Tuning

claims.statement: The pipeline can enhance KGR performance in both incomplete and general situations
Conventional KGR models' performances are limited when the KGs are severely incomplete
Recent LLM-enhanced KGR models require fine-tuning on open-source LLMs and are not applicable to closed-source LLMs
